question: How do I join the Office Hours or live workshop stream if I’m a student?
answer_agent:
If you’re a student, you **don’t join via the Zoom link** directly.

- **Watch the session on YouTube Live** on the DataTalksClub YouTube channel.
- The **video link is posted in the announcements channel** on Telegram and Slack before the session starts.
- To ask questions, use **Slido** — the link is pinned in the chat during the live stream.
- **Don’t post questions in the chat**, since they may be missed.

If you want, I can also point you to the FAQ about whether there are office hours for every module.
answer_orig:
The zoom link is only published to instructors/presenters/TAs.

Students participate via YouTube Live and submit questions to Slido (link is pinned in the chat when live). The video URL should be posted in the [announcements channel on Telegram and Slack](https://t.me/dezoomcamp) before it begins. You can also watch live on the DataTalksClub [YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/c/DataTalksClub).

Don’t post questions in chat as they may be missed if the room is very active.

question: Where should I begin if I’m just starting LLM Zoomcamp, and what pages/docs should I check first?
answer_agent:
If you’re just starting LLM Zoomcamp, the best place to begin is:

1. **LLM Zoomcamp docs**  
   https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/llm-zoomcamp/

2. **General Zoomcamp logistics docs**  
   https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/zoomcamp-logistics/

3. **LLM Zoomcamp GitHub repository**  
   https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp

Also, for course structure, deadlines, homework, and progress tracking, check the **course management platform**:  
https://courses.datatalks.club/llm-zoomcamp-2026/

A good workflow is:
- watch the lesson videos,
- work through the lesson notebooks/code,
- read the homework instructions on GitHub,
- submit answers on the course platform before the deadline.

If you want, I can also point you to the exact starting module for the current cohort.

Start with the [LLM Zoomcamp docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/llm-zoomcamp/), the [general Zoomcamp logistics docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/zoomcamp-logistics/), and the [LLM Zoomcamp GitHub repository](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp).

question: I don’t see my name on the leaderboard — how do I find my own entry?
answer_agent:
You should use the **“Jump to your record on the leaderboard”** link — your account is assigned a **random display name** by default, so your real name may not appear there.

If you want to check or change what name is shown:

- Click **“Edit Course Profile”**
- The **first field** is your **public display name/nickname** for the leaderboard
- The **second field** is your **official name** for the certificate

If you want, I can also explain how to make your leaderboard name match your Slack or GitHub name.
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!
tool_calls: [{'name': 'search', 'arguments': '{"query":"leaderboard my own entry find my name not showing"}'}]

question: Why does the homework content keep changing before it’s released?
answer_agent:
If the homework title contains `[DRAFT]`, it means the homework is not ready yet.

The homework is considered ready only when both of these are true:

- The homework form is open on the course management platform.
- The homework title does **not** contain `[DRAFT]`.

Until then, the content may still change. So if you work on it early, you do so at your own risk because the final version can be different.
